title: Leptochilus preslianus
intro: Leptochilus preslianus, commonly known as Preslie's polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Leptochilus preslianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leptochilus preslianus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.

As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Leptochilus preslianus is closely related to other plants in this family, such as the poinsettia and the spurge.
